title: What will the world be like in Q
display_title: What will the world be like in Q
wikitext:
[]: What will the world be like in "Q?"             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 10:35 pm After seeing nearly all of Tokyo 3 and the Geofront completely destroyed and possibly thousands of citizens left dead, what will become of Japan? For some reason, I'm thinking that Seele will take over the Geofront, as Gendo wasn't supposed to "awaken" Unit 01 yet, and the remains of Tokyo 3, with Kawrou as their enforcer. This was just the first thing that came into my mind, and that the rest of the survivors will have to ban together to survive. I know this sounds rather cliched and prison-movie-ish, but who knows? This is just my vision.

[]:             Quote Post by JimJam! » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:16 pm No more Toji, Kensuke, Hikari, and friends :( I don't know, it feels like 2.22's Third Impact is equivalent to Armisael's death in NGE where almost all Tokyo citizens are moved...

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:36 pm JimJam! wrote: No more Toji, Kensuke, Hikari, and friends :(

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 24, 2010 4:00 am Or ever.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 4:07 am AuraTwilight wrote: Or ever.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jul 24, 2010 5:05 am JimJam! wrote: No more Toji, Kensuke, (...), and friends!

[]:             Quote Post by Tycho » Sat Jul 24, 2010 6:59 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Or ever.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 7:39 pm Tycho wrote: (with seele maybe taking the role of gendo)

[]:             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Sat Jul 24, 2010 9:17 pm I don't even think Keel and his unhandsome lot of monoliths are going to do that, it would be a complete mess... My bet is that Keel's gonna do the same ol'thing he did in EoE, blowing the people of Nerv up.

[]:             Quote Post by JimJam! » Sat Jul 24, 2010 9:52 pm Isn't Kaworu's identity as an angel already exposed to NERV personnels at the end of 2.0? I mean he has this floating gimmick and halo on his eva at the end.... though Shinji and his Unit-01 does this too.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jul 24, 2010 10:20 pm That seems to be the powers of Unit 06, not Kaworu himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sun Jul 25, 2010 6:56 pm He's been seen half-naked on the moon too.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Jul 25, 2010 7:21 pm Rodeo: Excuse me! He was buck naked! And that is just nudism, not angelic powers... Besides If I recall right, in 2.0, when he activates the vacumm-skin-tighting mechanism of his suit, his hair moves as if air where being expelled from it, and there's also that "shuuuup" soud of air moving... He's no angel to me until he activates and Eva outside the entry plug...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Jul 26, 2010 11:50 am arael wrote: Rodeo: Excuse me! He was buck naked! And that is just nudism, not angelic powers... Besides If I recall right, in 2.0, when he activates the vacumm-skin-tighting mechanism of his suit, his hair moves as if air where being expelled from it, and there's also that "shuuuup" soud of air moving... He's no angel to me until he activates and Eva outside the entry plug...

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Mon Jul 26, 2010 12:37 pm arael wrote: Ibuki: One of the pilots is hurt! Ritsuko: he'll not be able to pilot! Keel: give him an enema!

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Jul 26, 2010 12:39 pm ^^Even more peculiar is that Kaworu is able to move in a manner that would be natural on Earth, despite the low gravity. This must be symbolic somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Jul 26, 2010 7:16 pm My big hope is the world of 3.0 is going to bleak and not offer much hope until the very end. The Rebuild films have mostly corresponded to the tone of the episodes from the series they are adapting. 1.0 took this to the extreme by pretty much being a slightly condensed version of the first 6 episodes. 2.0 covered twice as many episodes and corresponding to the TV show the first half of the film (roughly Episodes 8-15ish, we can disregard Jet Alone) was rather light hearted, jovial, and loaded with fan service. The second half of the film (corresponding to Episodes 16-19) was certainly more action packed and darker in tone. Thus, if 3.0 is adapting Episodes 20-24, the film should be very bleak and unsettling in it's tone. I'm hoping (against hope probably) that 3.0 will feature no goofy fan service moments and just be 100 minutes of emotional warfare with some truly astounding action sequences. You just know at some point in the film Eva 06 is going to fight one of the other Evas and that will be a showstopper moment! Eva Vs. Eva Rebuild style. Complete with amazing music slightly marred by horrid Engrish vocals if you pay attention to them. Anywho, all I really want out 3.0 is for it to be the best entry in this Rebuild franchise. And as long as Anno & Co focus on the characters and relationships they've established (i.e. Toji & Kensuke have no affiliation with NERV and thus will never be pilots and there are no NEWer characters added) there's a very good chance this will be the best Rebuild yet. Summer 2011 is a while off, huh?

[]:             Quote Post by Drew » Wed Jul 28, 2010 2:59 am Dirac-licious? Honestly, I think Tokyo-3 is done like dinner. I would assume that with the GeoFront exposed, there will be a ton of JSSDF uselessly set to defend the edge of the exposed GeoFront, and all surviving civillians from the shelters will be forcibly evacuated. Judging from the trailer, another angel attack is probably imminent, since the NERV high command appears to decide to do the humane thing and heal up Asuka. Hopefully Third Impact is successfully initiated and we get to see some of the effects of that as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Adamski » Thu Jul 29, 2010 1:14 am I noticed that Rebuild seemed to be paying a lot more attention to the destructive robot battles. When the three pilots took down the kamikaze angel (Someone remind me of it's name?) an entire portion of the city flooded with blood. Power lines were shown being torn down, major roads were crushed underfoot, etc. I don't know if anyone else noticed this, but one scene showing the aftermath of this battle showed the undersea research institute, the clear blue water that was kept safe from the read sea around it had angel blood splashed all in it, possibly poisoning all the sea life that the characters were so impressed with. Just something I thought was important to note...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Jul 29, 2010 6:59 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: 2.0 covered twice as many episodes and corresponding to the TV show the first half of the film (roughly Episodes 8-15ish, we can disregard Jet Alone) was rather light hearted, jovial, and loaded with fan service. The second half of the film (corresponding to Episodes 16-19) was certainly more action packed and darker in tone.
